evidence.network: GET /api/v1/strategies -> 200, response contains strategies array with 'chan_theory'

steps.expected: JSON with strategy, timeframe, code, params
steps.observed: Response with strategy='chan_theory', timeframe='1d', code (string with full Chan Theory YAML), params (2 param objects: min_bars_between, min_hub_pens)
200 with {running: true, progress: 0.0, status: 'running', status_msg: 'Backtest started: chan_theory (2025-11-03 to 2026-03-24)'}

assertions.title: Poll backtest progress and completion

steps.observed: POST response showed running=true with progress=0.0

steps.expected: running=true with progress or running=false when done
steps.observed: running=false, progress=1.0, backtest_result populated — backtest completed very fast (<3s)
backtest_result contains full strategy results with 7 trades and all performance metrics

assertions.issues: Backtest completed so quickly that the running=true intermediate state was only visible in the POST response, not during GET polling. This is functionally correct but means progress tracking cannot be observed for this data size.

steps.observed: profit_total=0.009293, profit_total_abs=9293.27, max_drawdown=-0.418272, sharpe=0.214803, sortino=0.276643, winrate=0.428571, trade_count=7, max_drawdown_abs=-698293.87 — all numeric, none null/NaN

evidence.network: All 8 required metrics present in backtest_result.strategy.chan_theory

assertions.title: Daily P&L data for equity curve

steps.expected: data array with entries containing date, abs_profit, rel_profit, starting_balance, trade_count; >= 30 entries
steps.observed: data array with 94 entries, all required fields present. First entry: 2025-11-03 with 0 profit. Last entry: 2026-03-24 with abs_profit=9293.27

evidence.network: GET /api/v1/daily -> 200, 94 entries with all required fields

steps.expected: trades array with >= 1 entry, each with pair, open_date, close_date, open_rate, close_rate, profit_abs, profit_ratio
steps.observed: trades array with 7 entries. First trade: pair=000630, open_date=2025-12-02, close_date=2026-03-24, open_rate=5.41, close_rate=5.81, profit_abs=21651.65, profit_ratio=0.072241

evidence.network: GET /api/v1/trades -> 200, 7 trades with all required fields

steps.expected: Array with >= 1 entry including strategy, filename, backtest_start_ts, backtest_end_ts
steps.observed: Array with 129 entries (previous runs accumulated). First entry: strategy=chan_theory, filename=backtest-result-chan_theory-2025-11-03-2026-03-24-129.json, backtest_start_ts=1762099200000, backtest_end_ts=1774281600000

evidence.network: GET /api/v1/backtest/history -> 200, 129 entries with all required fields

frictions.description: Backtest completes almost instantly for the given timerange/strategy, making it impossible to observe the running=true intermediate state via polling. The running=true was only visible in the POST response.
frictions.resolution: Validated running=true from POST response and running=false with populated results from GET response. Both states were observed.

frictions.description: Backtest history endpoint returned 129 entries from previous test runs, indicating history accumulates across runs without cleanup.
frictions.resolution: Not a blocker — the assertion only requires >= 1 entry. History accumulation is expected behavior.

summary: Tested 8 assertions: all 8 passed. Strategy listing returns chan_theory, strategy details include all required fields (name, timeframe, code, params). Backtest starts successfully, completes with 7 trades, and all performance metrics (profit_total, max_drawdown, sharpe, sortino, winrate, trade_count, etc.) are numeric. Daily P&L has 94 entries, trades endpoint returns 7 trades with full details, and backtest history persists across runs.
